title: Fix busted assignment
body:
I'm seeing incorrect sim results on a struct which is assigned partially in an always_comb and partially via a port map.

The problem appears to be that `what_d` is assigned from bit 0 of some trace / temp var:
```
    1:2: ASSIGNW 0x5555572a3ae0 <e3467#> {f39aq} @dt=0x55555726aa80@(G/w1)
    1:2:1: SEL 0x555557268180 <e3464#> {f39bd} @dt=0x55555726aa80@(G/w1) widthConst=1
    1:2:1:1: VARREF 0x555557267110 <e3458#> {f33ao} @dt=0x5555572abec0@(G/w34)  __VdfgTraceDriver_haf325e27_0_0 [RV] <- VAR 0x55555734eb40 <e3445#> {f33ao} @dt=0x5555572abec0@(G/w34)  __VdfgTraceDriver_haf325e27_0_0 [INTERNAL] MODULETEMP
    1:2:1:2: CONST 0x5555573645a0 <e3459#> {f39bd} @dt=0x5555572ab8c0@(G/w32)  32'h0
```

but the only time that trace / temp var is assigned we do so starting at bit 1:

```
    1:2: ASSIGNW 0x5555572a3cc0 <e3507#> {f37ay} @dt=0x555557268000@(G/w33)
    1:2:1: VARREF 0x555557267ad0 <e3504#> {f37ba} @dt=0x555557268000@(G/w33)  __VdfgRegularize_hc866feca_0_0 [RV] <- VAR 0x55555734ec60 <e3448#> {f37ba} @dt=0x555557268000@(G/w33)  __VdfgRegularize_hc866feca_0_0 MODULETEMP
    1:2:2: SEL 0x555557268780 <e3505#> {f37ay} @dt=0x555557268000@(G/w33) widthConst=33
    1:2:2:1: VARREF 0x555557267a00 <e3496#> {f37ay} @dt=0x5555572abec0@(G/w34)  __VdfgTraceDriver_haf325e27_0_0 [LV] => VAR 0x55555734eb40 <e3445#> {f33ao} @dt=0x5555572abec0@(G/w34)  __VdfgTraceDriver_haf325e27_0_0 [INTERNAL] MODULETEMP
    1:2:2:2: CONST 0x555557364780 <e3497#> {f37ay} @dt=0x5555572ab8c0@(G/w32)  32'h1
```

So far I've been unable to capture this without referencing the VCD.  However, it definitely also impacts sim behavior as some failing tests of ours are how I got here in the first place.

I'm just getting started trying to figure out what's going on here, but since I see `dfg` again, cc @gezalore who might find it more immediately obvious.

Note, you can run `t/t_struct_split_assign.py -DSPLIT_VAR` to see the correct behavior / VCD.
